care.summary: A stunning tropical plant with glossy heart-shaped leaves and long-lasting red, pink, or white spathe flowers.
care.light: Bright indirect light; too little light reduces flowering
care.water: Keep soil lightly moist; water when top inch feels dry. Use distilled water
care.soil: Well-draining aroid or orchid mix with peat and perlite
care.humidity: High (60%+); mist regularly or use a humidifier
care.fertilizer: Dilute high-phosphorus fertilizer every 4 weeks spring through fall
care.toxicity: Toxic if ingested; keep away from pets and children
common_issues: No flowers — insufficient light or nutrients
Brown leaf tips from dry air or tap water
Yellow leaves from overwatering or cold drafts
Root rot in heavy, poorly draining soil
tips: Wipe leaves monthly to keep pores dust-free for better blooming
Use distilled or rainwater — anthuriums are sensitive to chemicals
Remove spent flowers at the base to encourage new blooms
Repot every 2 years with fresh aroid mix
